cancer_history_summary:
Age: 19  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Osteosarcoma (high‑grade conventional) of the distal right femur  
Histology: High‑grade conventional osteosarcoma  
Current extent: Oligometastatic – solitary pulmonary metastasis in the right lower lobe (2.0 × 1.8 cm) identified on 06/25/2025 CT  

Biomarkers: No molecular/genetic results reported to date; comprehensive sarcoma‑focused NGS ordered (pending).  

Treatment history:  
# Approx. 08/2024: Limb‑sparing surgical resection of distal right femur with endoprosthetic reconstruction (curative intent)  
# 09/2024‑04/2025: Adjuvant MAP chemotherapy (methotrexate, doxorubicin, cisplatin) – six cycles, completed without major complication (disease‑free on imaging up to 06/22/2025)  
# Early 2025 (exact dates not recorded): Two cycles of ifosfamide/etoposide administered (preceding surveillance CT)  
# 06/25/2025‑present: Observation of solitary right lower‑lobe pulmonary nodule; management under consideration includes video‑assisted thoracoscopic wedge resection versus stereotactic body radiation therapy (SBRT); biopsy and further systemic therapy planning pending
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal dysfunction, liver dysfunction, HIV infection, or hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 68  
Sex: Male  

Cancer type: Cutaneous malignant melanoma (left upper extremity primary)  
Histology: Malignant melanoma, cutaneous origin  
Current extent: Metastatic (bilateral pulmonary metastases); no extrathoracic disease identified on latest imaging  

Biomarkers: BRAFV600E mutation-positive; NRAS wild-type; LDH modestly elevated at 285 U/L on 6/22/2025 (subsequently 185 U/L on 6/23/2025)  

Treatment history:  
# Surgical resection with wide local excision and sentinel lymph node dissection (including removal of 2 of 3 positive nodes with extracapsular extension) – performed circa 2022 (exact date not recorded)  
# Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks – initiated post‑operative, continued for 12 months, completed approximately May 2024; tolerated well except Grade 2 dermatitis at month 6, fully resolved  
# Ipilimumab 3 mg/kg IV + Nivolumab 1 mg/kg IV – commenced 22 Jun 2025; scheduled every 3 weeks for up to 4 induction cycles  
 • Cycle 1: 22 Jun 2025 – tolerated with Grade 1 pruritus only  
 • Cycle 2: 13 Jul 2025 – uneventful; imaging 6 weeks after start showed ~33% reduction in pulmonary nodules (Partial Response per RECIST v1.1)  
 • Cycle 3: late July 2025 – resulted in Grade 3 immune‑mediated colitis requiring hospitalisation; ipilimumab permanently discontinued afterward  
# Nivolumab maintenance – transitioned to nivolumab monotherapy 240 mg IV every 2 weeks beginning after cessation of ipilimumab (circa August 2025) and continues indefinitely pending disease progression or intolerable toxicity
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal dysfunction, hepatic dysfunction, or HIV/hepatitis infection in the available records.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 55  
Sex: Female  

Cancer type: Ovarian cancer  
Histology: Low‑grade serous carcinoma (LGSC)  

Current extent: Recurrent/advanced disease confined to peritoneal implants and regional retroperitoneal lymph nodes (pelvic sidewall implants, 2 cm para‑aortic node); no extra‑abdominal or bony metastases evident on imaging as of 24 Jun 2025.  

Biomarkers: KRAS p.G12D dominant clone (VAF ≈45%), co‑existent NRAS Q61K subclone (low VAF), CCNE1 three‑fold copy number gain; CA‑125 trending upward (85 U/mL → 118 U/mL → 210 U/mL reported 22 Jun 2025; later recorded 62 U/mL on 23 Jun 2025).  

Treatment history:  
# Approx 2023 (patient age 53): Total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo‑oophorectomy, comprehensive surgical staging.  
# Apr 2024 (completed 14 months prior to 22 Jun 2025): Adjuvant chemotherapy – carboplatin AUC 5 + paclitaxel 175 mg/m² IV, 4 cycles; tolerated with Grade 2 nausea (controlled).  
# Mar 2025‑present: Endocrine therapy – letrozole 2.5 mg orally once daily, initiated due to rising CA‑125 and stable residual peritoneal disease; ongoing. Best response: Initial symptomatic improvement and stable disease on CT (01 Jul 2025 impression), however PET/CT on 22 Jun 2025 revealed new FDG‑avid pelvic sidewall lesions and a para‑aortic node suggesting progression (classified as platinum‑refractory LGSC). Subsequent contrast‑enhanced CT on 24 Jun 2025 showed those implants remained stable in size.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant renal dysfunction (Cr 0.85 mg/dL), hepatic dysfunction (AST/ALT within normal limits), or HIV/Hepatitis infection documented in the available records.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 45  
Sex: Male  

Cancer type: T‑cell lymphoblastic lymphoma (primary site anterior mediastinum)  
Histology: Lymphoblastic lymphoma, TdT positive, T‑cell lineage (CD3⁺, CD7⁺, CD20⁻, CD19⁻, CD22⁻)  
Current extent: Disseminated/metastatic disease with residual mediastinal mass, bilateral pulmonary nodules, multiple hepatic lesions, probable splenic and bone‑marrow involvement (Stage IV biologically)  

Biomarkers:  
- Immunophenotype: CD3⁺, CD7⁺, TdT⁺, CD20⁻, CD19⁻, CD22⁻  
- Genomics: NOTCH1 V1578F mutation, PTEN loss, JAK3 V678M mutation, TAL1‑ST5 fusion  

Treatment history:  
# Pre‑2025 (dates not explicitly recorded): Front‑line Hyper‑CVAD ± high‑dose methotrexate/cytarabine – 4 cycles; achieved partial radiographic response (mediastinal mass reduced from ≈9 cm to ≈4 cm).  
# Pre‑2025 (following Hyper‑CVAD): Salvage nelarabine – 2 cycles; produced stable disease.  
# Early June 2025 → completion by 23 Jun 2025: Palliative external‑beam radiation to mediastinum, total dose 30 Gy in 10 fractions; post‑treatment CT showed ∼70 % reduction of mediastinal mass to ≈3 cm.  
# 22 Jun 2025 – 26 Jun 2025: Prednisone 40 mg PO daily × 5 days with taper (initiated 22 Jun 2025).  
# Approx. 05 May 2025 (six weeks before 22 Jun 2025): Therapeutic anticoagulation for right popliteal/deep‑vein thrombosis – enoxaparin 1 mg/kg SC q12h, subsequently transitioned to apixaban 5 mg PO BID (continuing).  
# Planned after 22 Jun 2025: Bortezomib 1.0 mg/m² SC on Days 1, 8, 15 of each 28‑day cycle (dose reduction because of Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy); intention to discontinue vincristine after first cycle if neuropathy worsens. (Start date not yet reached.)
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence:
- Liver dysfunction: Grade 3 transaminitis attributed to hepatic infiltration documented on 22 Jun 2025 (“AST/ALT >5 × ULN”) and reiterated on 25 Jun 2025 (“grade 3 transaminitis”).  
- No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal dysfunction, HIV infection, or hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 76  
Sex: Female  
Cancer type: Ovarian cancer  
Histology: High‑grade serous carcinoma  
Current extent: Metastatic (FIGO Stage IV), presently stable disease with residual hepatic metastases showing post‑treatment changes and a fibrotic para‑aortic nodal deposit; no ascites or new lesions.  
Biomarkers: CA‑125 48 U/mL (stable vs prior 44 U/mL)  

Treatment history:  
# 2024 (date of diagnosis): Diagnosis of FIGO Stage IV high‑grade serous ovarian carcinoma (metastatic).  
# 2024‑2025: Systemic platinum‑taxane‐based chemotherapy (presumed per standard of care; specific agents and dates not detailed in record).  
# 2024‑present: Rucaparib 600 mg orally twice daily, continuously administered; tolerating grade 2 anemia/fatigue, no new toxicities.  
# March 2025 (≈3 months before 6/20/2025): Percutaneous radiofrequency ablation of dominant hepatic metastasis (initial size 4.2 cm; post‑procedure size 3.8 cm with central necrosis).  
# March 2025: Stereotactic body radiation therapy (SBRT) to solitary para‑aortic lymph node, 30 Gy delivered in 5 fractions; resulting in fibrosis with no measurable enhancement.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant renal dysfunction (creatinine 0.84 mg/dL), clinically important liver dysfunction (AST 38 U/L, ALT 42 U/L, alk phos 112 U/L), HIV infection, or hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 70  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Prostate cancer  
Histology: Adenocarcinoma, Gleason 9 (4+5)  
Current extent: Metastatic castration‑resistant (bone‑only disease, no visceral metastases)  

Biomarkers: Castrate testosterone <20 ng/dL (most recent assay); PSA rose from 0.4 → 2.6 ng/mL over 6 weeks indicating biochemical progression; pending next‑generation sequencing for DDR genes (BRCA1/2, ATM, PALB2).  

Treatment history:  
# Date unspecified – Prior to 2025: Radical prostatectomy with extended pelvic lymphadenectomy (curative intent).  
# Ongoing since initiation (continuous): Leuprolide depot intramuscular injections (androgen deprivation therapy).  
# ~mid‑2024 to 6/19/2025: Abiraterone acetate 1000 mg PO daily plus prednisone 5 mg BID (discontinued 6/19/2025 due to biochemical progression).  
# 6/19/2025‑present: Enzalutamide 160 mg PO daily (started 6/19/2025, continues).  
# Planned (not yet started): Docetaxel 75 mg/m² IV q21 days ± prednisolone 5 mg BID; Radium‑223 dichloride for symptomatic bone‑dominant disease; Genomic sequencing of archival tumor or ctDNA.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal dysfunction, liver dysfunction, HIV infection, or hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 63  
Sex: Unspecified  
Cancer type: Small‑cell lung carcinoma (extensive‑stage)  
Histology: Small‑cell carcinoma  
Current extent: Progressive disease with new solitary hepatic metastasis (segment VII, 1.6 cm) while other extracranial sites remain stable; no intracranial disease.  
Biomarkers: None reported  

Treatment history:  
# Early 2025 (approximately January–April): carboplatin AUC 5 + etoposide 100 mg/m² Day 1‑3 q21 days × 4 cycles; concurrent palliative external beam radiation to L3 (single 8 Gy fraction). Outcome: partial response on cycle 2 CT (~30% decrease), subsequently stable disease; toxicities included Grade 3 neutropenia (treated with filgrastim), Grade 3 thrombocytopenia requiring transfusion, and Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy.  
# April 2025 – June 2025: lurbinectedin 3.2 mg/m² IV q21 days; completed at least 5 cycles (dose reduced after Grade 3 neutropenia). Outcomes: modest reduction of left adrenal lesion, stable right femoral lytic lesion; developed Grade 2 anemia (Hb 9.0 g/dL) managed with oral iron and recurrent neutropenia necessitating dose adjustment. Restaging PET‑CT performed 7 days prior to 2025‑06‑25 demonstrated new hepatic metastasis meeting RECIST criteria for progressive disease.  
# Planned starting June 2025: temozolomide 200 mg/m² orally Days 1‑5 q28 days (proposed fourth‑line therapy); not yet initiated.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence:
- Mild hepatic dysfunction attributed to metastatic liver involvement (documented 2025‑06‑25).  
- No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases (brain MRI repeatedly negative).  
- No documentation of congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, clinically significant renal dysfunction, HIV infection, or active hepatitis.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 62  
Sex: Female  
Cancer type: Primary central nervous system glioblastoma, right parietal location (IDH‑wildtype)  
Histology: Glioblastoma, WHO Grade IV, IDH‑wildtype  
Current extent: Recurrent/progressive disease with new non‑enhancing infiltrative focus (~2 cm) in right posterior parietal lobe; stable left temporal resection cavity; no extracranial metastases identified  

Biomarkers: IDH‑wildtype (documented)  

Treatment history:  
# Pre‑2025 (exact dates unspecified): Surgical resection of left temporal lesion (resection cavity noted) and prior intervention on right parietal lesion; subsequent focal radiation therapy (inferred from discussion of radiation‑induced changes).  
# Pre‑2025 (experimental phase, dates unspecified): Combination therapy with investigational PDGFRα inhibitor plus temozolomide; discontinued due to disease progression.  
# Early 2025–mid 2025: Intravenous bevacizumab 10 mg/kg every 14 days initiated (specific start date not recorded). Achieved transient symptomatic benefit; therapy paused owing to development of grade 3 proteinuria.  
# 6/25/2025–present: Bevacizumab monotherapy resumed at 10 mg/kg intravenously every 14 days (ongoing as of last note).
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: Evidence of renal dysfunction – grade 3 proteinuria attributed to bevacizumab therapy observed in mid‑2025, necessitating temporary drug interruption. No documentation of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, hepatic dysfunction, HIV, or hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 66  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Breast cancer (right-sided)  
Histology: Invasive lobular carcinoma, hormone‑receptor positive (ER⁺/PR⁺), HER2‑low  
Current extent: Metastatic (known hepatic lesion in segment IV and multiple osseous metastases in thoracic spine; disease classified as stable/responding)  
Biomarkers: PIK3CA E545K mutation; ESR1 D538G mutation; HER2‑low (no HER2 amplification)

Best response: partial response per RECIST v1.1; most recent imaging shows reduction of hepatic metastasis from 1.8 cm to ~1.5 cm and stable skeletal disease.

general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, clinically significant renal or hepatic dysfunction, HIV infection, or chronic viral hepatitis documented in the available records.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 34  
Sex: Male  

Cancer type: Gastrointestinal neuroendocrine tumour (appendiceal origin)  
Histology: Well‑differentiated NET, Grade 2 (Ki‑67 ≈ 8%)  
Current extent: Metastatic to liver; disease stable on current therapy with two small hepatic lesions (segment III scar ≤0.6 cm, segment VI 1.0 cm) and no detectable extra‑hepatic disease  

Biomarkers: Somatostatin receptor‑2 positive (⁶⁸Ga‑DOTATATE SUVmax ≈ 12); Ki‑67 ~ 8%  

Treatment history:  
# Approx. age 24 (2023): Appendectomy – curative intent for stage IA (T1 N0 M0) appendiceal NET  
# Ages 25‑27 (approx. 2024‑2026): Isolated peritoneal nodules treated with temozolomide + bevazicumab → partial response  
# Following peritoneal response (circa 2026): Initiated octreotide LAR 30 mg IM q28 days (ongoing) after confirming SSTR2 positivity  
# Pre‑age 31 (prior to 2025‑06‑19): Peptide receptor radionuclide therapy (Lu‑177‑DOTATATE PRRT) – achieved sustained disease control except for eventual hepatic progression  
# Approximately March 2025: Started everolimus 10 mg PO daily combined with continued octreotide LAR 30 mg q28 days (off‑label) → stable disease per RECIST on MRI 3 months later (June 2025)  

Best responses: Partial response to temozolomide/bevazicumab; regression of treated hepatic lesion in segment III to ≤0.5 cm post‑PRRT; stable disease on everolimus + octreotide as of June 2025.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant renal dysfunction, significant liver dysfunction, HIV infection, or hepatitis infection documented in the available excerpts.
